name: Custom Unique Identifier (Custom UID)
body:
[{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"You can implement custom unique identifiers to enable advanced contact management scenarios where your organization requires specific identification systems beyond standard email-based tracking. This helps optimize marketing effectiveness when dealing with shared email addresses or complex customer hierarchies, while ensuring accurate reporting and personalization across your email campaigns."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"Custom UID configuration significantly impacts how your Marketing Cloud account operates across contacts, segments, journeys, and reporting, making it essential to understand these changes thoroughly before implementation since the configuration cannot be reversed."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"bold":true,"text":"Unique Identifier"},{"text":" ("},{"text":"UID","bold":true},{"text":") is an alphanumeric string unique to each contact and, thereby, can be used as an identifying attribute for a contact. Generally, Marketing Cloud automatically generates the UID, which is then mostly used internally within the system. "}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"However, there are scenarios where you may want to provide a unique identifier for your contacts, and you can do so using the custom UID feature."}]},{"type":"callout","children":[{"type":"callout-item","children":[{"bold":true,"text":"Important:"},{"text":" To avail the custom UID feature, your account will need to be specifically configured, and these changes cannot be reverted. The custom UID feature comes with certain limitations, and therefore, please consult your customer success manager thoroughly before opting for the feature."}]}],"appearance":"alert"},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"This article covers the changes and limitations in various Marketing Cloud features for custom UID-based accounts:"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Contacts"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h3","children":[{"text":"Creating Contact Manually"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"When manually creating a new contact, you must provide a unique UID for the contact. The email address or phone number is a must only if you want to add the contact to a list. Also, the email address and phone number don’t have to be unique."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"The rest of the contact creation process remains the same as in the regular accounts. See "},{"type":"a","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Creating-and-Managing-Contacts-New","target":"_blank","children":[{"text":"Creating and Managing Contacts","bold":true}]},{"text":" for detailed information."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"type":"rich_embed_card","children":[{"text":""}],"dataSourceType":"image","url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5miRMAR.jpg","embedData":{"url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5miRMAR.jpg","name":"","embedType":"image","float":"none","height":427,"width":699}},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h3","children":[{"text":"Viewing and Managing Contacts"}]}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"The Contacts index page (in Navigation > CONTACTS) contains an additional column for displaying the custom UID. You can click the UID to go to the contact’s 360-degree overview. See "},{"type":"a","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Contact-360-Overview-New","target":"_blank","children":[{"text":"Contact 360-Degree Overview","bold":true}]},{"text":". "}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"type":"rich_embed_card","children":[{"text":""}],"dataSourceType":"image","url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5mivMAB.jpg","embedData":{"url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5mivMAB.jpg","name":"","embedType":"image","float":"none","height":398,"width":699}},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h3","children":[{"text":"Importing Contacts"}]}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"For custom UID-based accounts, the UID must be unique for importing contacts, while the email address or phone number need not be unique."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"During the import, you’ll see a field for UID in the Mappings section. Map your account’s UID field with the UID column in the import file. The UID provided in the import file determines whether a new contact is created or an existing contact is updated."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"The rest of the import process remains the same as in the regular accounts. For more information, see "},{"type":"a","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Creating-and-Managing-Contacts-New","target":"_blank","children":[{"text":"Creating and Managing Contacts","bold":true}]},{"text":". "}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"type":"rich_embed_card","children":[{"text":""}],"dataSourceType":"image","url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5mkcMAB.jpg","embedData":{"url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5mkcMAB.jpg","name":"","embedType":"image","float":"none","height":334,"width":703}},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Segments"}]}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"You can query a Segment using UID. Also, note that a UID-based segment query could potentially return a higher number of contacts than the actual campaign sends. This is because some contacts could be missing an email address or phone number."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"To learn about Segments, see "},{"type":"rich_embed_inline","dataSourceType":"url","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Introduction-to-Segments","embedData":{"body":"The idea behind segmentation is to send personalized emails that are relevant and engaging to your contacts. With the help of segments, you can send curated campaigns that help you boost your online sales in the long run. Although grouping contacts based on certain criteria can be a tricky task, if done properly, it can…","photoUrl":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/T5UGEWUOKGD6/title-bar-logo.png","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Introduction-to-Segments","embedType":"link","name":"Introduction to Segments - Maropost Community"},"children":[{"text":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Introduction-to-Segments"}]},{"text":"."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Journeys"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"In a custom UID-based account, when using the API Event trigger in Journey, you can use the UID to trigger a contact’s journey."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"If the API Event trigger is used to trigger a Journey using an email address, then all UIDs associated with the email address will go through the journey. For example, if Jane and Bob share the email address "},{"type":"a","url":"mailto:millers@me.com","target":"_blank","children":[{"text":"millers@me.com"}]},{"text":", and the API Event trigger is used to trigger a Journey for "},{"type":"a","url":"mailto:millers@me.com","target":"_blank","children":[{"text":"millers@me.com"}]},{"text":", then both Jane's and Bob’s UID will go through the Journey."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"To learn about Journeys, see "},{"type":"rich_embed_inline","dataSourceType":"url","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Introduction-to-Journeys","embedData":{"body":"You can use the following widgets to create a Journey: * Journey Triggers: Use the Journey Trigger widget to define the rules by which a contact enters a Journey. Every trigger has a different function and can be used to design a creative Journey that will engage your contacts, and automate your email marketing process.…","photoUrl":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/T5UGEWUOKGD6/title-bar-logo.png","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Introduction-to-Journeys","embedType":"link","name":"Introduction to Journeys - Maropost Cart REST API","bold":true}]},{"text":""}]}]}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"For Keap, Zapier, Salesforce, and Magento integrations, the exact column matching the "},{"text":"UID","bold":true},{"text":" must be"},{"text":" ","bold":true},{"text":"selected during the integration setup to import contacts through the integration."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"For the Abandoned Cart REST API, the UID parameter must be included in the API call."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Acquisition Forms"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"For custom UID-based accounts, both UID and email address fields are mandatory. Therefore, you’ll need to create a script to auto-populate the UID value."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"To learn about Acquisition Forms, see "},{"type":"rich_embed_inline","dataSourceType":"url","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Acquisition-Forms","embedData":{"body":"The easy-to-create forms can be designed to match your brand and can be customized to suit your purpose. The form and design settings give you control over how your form will look and where it will appear on the screen. The options such as ReCaptcha and Double Opt-in functionalities enable you to enforce security and…","photoUrl":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/T5UGEWUOKGD6/title-bar-logo.png","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Acquisition-Forms","embedType":"link","name":"Acquisition Forms - Maropost Community"},"children":[{"text":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Acquisition-Forms"}]},{"text":"."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"type":"rich_embed_card","children":[{"text":""}],"dataSourceType":"image","url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5mhnMAB.jpg","embedData":{"url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5mhnMAB.jpg","name":"","embedType":"image","float":"none","height":318,"width":701}}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Email Content"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"When previewing email content, you can select a contact using their UID. To learn about previewing content, see "},{"type":"a","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Previewing-Content-in-Content-Editors","target":"_blank","children":[{"text":"Previewing Content in Content Editors","bold":true}]},{"text":"."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"type":"rich_embed_card","children":[{"text":""}],"dataSourceType":"image","url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5ldRMAR.jpg","embedData":{"url":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/image.05TJ100000Y5ldRMAR.jpg","name":"","embedType":"image","float":"none","height":408,"width":563}}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Preference Management"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"In the case of a shared email address, if a contact unsubscribes, then all contacts (UIDs) sharing the email address will also be unsubscribed."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"To learn about preference management, see "},{"type":"rich_embed_inline","dataSourceType":"url","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Preference-Management-Overview","embedData":{"body":"Each preference page is intended to provide your contacts with a specific option to manage their preference, for example, managing which lists they want to subscribe to, updating their profile information, a single-click unsubscribing from all lists, reporting spam. The Preference Management Index page (In Navigation >…","photoUrl":"https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/T5UGEWUOKGD6/title-bar-logo.png","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Preference-Management-Overview","embedType":"link","name":"Preference Management: Overview - Maropost Community"},"children":[{"text":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Preference-Management-Overview"}]},{"text":"."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":""}]},{"type":"h2","children":[{"text":"Reports and Analytics"}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"Email engagement metrics are counted based on UID. When an email address is associated with multiple UIDs, the activity from separate UIDs is counted as separate for reporting. For example, if Bob (UID 1) and Jane (UID 2) share an email address, and both click the email once each, then the email address will have two unique clicks."}]},{"type":"p","children":[{"text":"To learn about reports, see "},{"type":"rich_embed_inline","dataSourceType":"url","url":"https://galaxy.maropost.com/s/article/Reports-and-Analytics-Overview","embedData":{"body":"These reports can give you valuable insight into how your campaigns are performing, the results of your A|B split tests, how contacts are engaging with your sent campaigns, as well as other important information.

In the Navigation, go to ANALYTICS, and select the desired report.



 

Campaign Reports

The campaign reports help you track the performance of your email campaigns. Each report generated for an email campaign contains the following tabs:

Dashboard: Provides insights on how the email campaign is performing. You can see the delivery statistics (such as delivery rate, bounce rate), user engagement ( such as opens, clicks), revenue, and other important metrics.Overlay: Provides a click-map view of the email content displaying the links along with total clicks and percentage of the total clicks for each link.ISP: Provides insight on the performance of the email campaign based on Internet Service Providers (ISPs) of the recipients' email addresses.Details: Provides details of the email campaign.

For more information, see Email Campaign Reports.

 

Recurring Campaign Reports

The recurring campaign reports help you track the performance of your recurring campaigns. You can click the eye icon against each recurring campaign report and see the individual occurrence when the recurring campaign has been sent.

 

A/B Campaign Reports

The A|B campaign reports show the delivery and response data for each group of the A|B test as well as those of the final campaign sent based on the 'Winning' criteria.

For more information, see A/B Campaign Reports.

 

Custom Reports

The Custom Reports are summary reports that you can generate based on your choices. You have the option to filter the data to be included in the custom report if you want to focus on a narrower audience than those included in the original target audience. You can also set the option to have the report sent to any number of email addresses and also to have the report generated on a recurring basis.

For more information, see Custom Reports: Overview.

Currently, the following custom reports are supported:

Custom Campaign Reports – To learn how to create a custom Campaign report, see Custom Email Campaign Report.Custom SMS Campaign Reports – To learn how to create an SMS Campaign report, see Custom SMS Campaign Report.Deliverability Reports – To learn how to create a Deliverability report, see Deliverability Report.Growth and Attrition Reports – To learn how to create a Growth and Attrition report, see Growth & Attrition Report: Overview and Creating Growth & Attrition Reports.

 

Test Campaign Reports

The Test Campaign Reports show the same information as the Campaign Reports. The main difference between them is that the test campaign cannot be sent to more than 20 contacts.For more information, see Test Campaign Reports.

 

Website Reports

The Website Reports are displayed only if you are using Marketing Cloud's Web Tracking feature. These reports show you detail on web pages that have been viewed and how many times they have been viewed. The report also includes drill-down reports showing which contacts have viewed which pages and how many times they have viewed them.For more information, see Website Reports.

 

Journey Reports

The Journey Reports provide you with detailed information on how a particular journey is performing. On the index page, you can see all the journey reports along with their important statistics (such as sent, delivered, opens, clicks, bounces, total revenue, and last updated). By clicking the eye icon against a journey report, you can see the email campaigns within the journey. You can further click the campaign names to see the corresponding campaign reports.For more information, see Journey Reports. 

Transactional Campaign Reports

The Transactional Campaign Reports help you track the performance of your transactional campaigns. Each report generated for a transactional campaign contains the following three tabs:

Dashboard: Provides insights on how the transactional campaign is performing. You can see the delivery statistics (such as delivery rate, bounce rate), user engagement ( such as opens, clicks), revenue, and other important metrics.ISP: Provides insight on the performance of the email campaign based on Internet Service Providers (ISPs) of the recipients' email addresses.Details: Provides details of the transactional campaign.

For more information, see Transactional Campaign Reports. 

Log Inspector

The Log Inspector report shows the failed API calls.
